# Plan - Fix CI on PR #33 (terraform-module-testing)

## Context

PR #33 (`feat/terraform-module-testing`, repo `bgauduch/terraform-examples`) adds the live's
native `terraform test` example. CI had 3 red checks. Two are **already fixed and pushed**
(commit `b384bf0`, force-pushed):
- **Conventional commits** - commit body line was 120 chars; reflowed to <=100. GREEN.
- **terraform fmt / deferred-actions/02-after** - the latter was a transient 503 on trivy
  attestation download; the re-push re-ran it. GREEN.
- tflint findings in the example (missing `required_version`/provider version in
  `tests/setup/main.tf`, unused `enable_encryption`) - fixed. tflint now exits 0.
  Version constraints later corrected to root-style `~>` per HashiCorp best practice
  (root modules: `~>` lower+upper bound; reusable modules: `>=`). User also translated
  touched-file comments FR->EN.

**Remaining red check:** `validate + lint + scan (terraform-module-testing)` now fails at the
**trivy** step (it previously failed earlier at tflint, so trivy never ran). Single finding:

> **AWS-0132 (HIGH)** - `modules/s3-bucket/main.tf:7-11` - Bucket does not encrypt data with a
> customer managed key.

Root cause: the module makes SSE-KMS **conditional** (`count = kms_enabled ? 1 : 0`); with the
root's default vars (`enable_encryption=false`, `kms_key_arn=null`) the encryption resource is
absent, so trivy (static scan of the root default path) sees an unencrypted-by-CMK bucket and
gates the job (HIGH,CRITICAL -> exit 1). Confirmed locally with trivy 0.71.2. Only this one
finding (no whack-a-mole).

**Verified fact (both Explore agents):** AWS-0132 passes IFF the bucket has an always-attached
`aws_s3_bucket_server_side_encryption_configuration` with `sse_algorithm="aws:kms"` AND
`kms_master_key_id` resolving to a **non-null CMK** in the root's default eval. The sister
`deferred-actions/02-after` example passes because its root creates `aws_kms_key.root` and
always injects it.

## Decision (user-chosen)

Make the **module encrypt by default with a CMK** (secure-by-default at the module level, not
patched at the root), and **keep `enable_encryption`** (repurposed). The module **creates its
own CMK** when none is provided - the only way to satisfy AWS-0132 without the root supplying a
key.

New semantics:
- `enable_encryption` (default **true**) = "encrypt with a customer-managed key (CMK)".
  - `true` + `kms_key_arn` set -> use the provided CMK.
  - `true` + `kms_key_arn` null -> **module creates a dedicated `aws_kms_key`** and uses it.
  - `false` -> SSE-KMS with the **aws/s3 default managed key** (runtime path; trivy sees the
    default `true`, so the gate passes).
- The **conditional resource** (for the plan-assertion teaching point) moves from the encryption
  block to the **KMS key creation** (`aws_kms_key.this`, created only when no key is provided).
- Cross-variable validation + `expect_failures` demo is **preserved** by inverting the rule:
  `kms_key_arn` is forbidden when `enable_encryption=false`.

Comments in every touched file go **EN** (matches the user's FR->EN migration of this repo).

input.old_string: Comments in every touched file go **EN** (matches the user's FR->EN migration of this repo).
input.new_string:
Comments in every touched file go **EN** (matches the user's FR->EN migration of this repo).

## Changes by file

### `modules/s3-bucket/main.tf` (core)

Replace the `locals` + the conditional encryption resource. Add a conditional KMS key.

```hcl
locals {
  # Encrypt by default with a customer-managed key (CMK). With no kms_key_arn the
  # module creates its own CMK so the bucket is never left on the aws/s3 default key.
  create_kms_key = var.enable_encryption && var.kms_key_arn == null
  # Effective key feeding the SSE rule: caller-provided, else module-created, else
  # null -> bucket uses S3's default managed key (aws/s3) when encryption is off.
  effective_kms_key_arn = var.enable_encryption ? coalesce(var.kms_key_arn, one(aws_kms_key.this[*].arn)) : null
}

resource "aws_kms_key" "this" {
  count                   = local.create_kms_key ? 1 : 0
  description             = "Module-managed CMK for bucket ${var.bucket_name}"
  enable_key_rotation     = true
  deletion_window_in_days = 7
}

# aws_s3_bucket / public_access_block / versioning: UNCHANGED

resource "aws_s3_bucket_server_side_encryption_configuration" "this" {
  # Always attached (secure-by-default); count removed.
  bucket = aws_s3_bucket.this.id
  rule {
    apply_server_side_encryption_by_default {
      sse_algorithm     = "aws:kms"
      kms_master_key_id = local.effective_kms_key_arn # CMK arn, or null -> aws/s3 key
    }
    bucket_key_enabled = true
  }
}
```
`coalesce` is never all-null (the only-true branch guarantees one non-null arg). `aws` provider
>=5.0 already declared in `versions.tf` supports `aws_kms_key` - no version bump.

### `modules/s3-bucket/variables.tf`
- `enable_encryption`: default `false` -> **`true`**; description rewritten (CMK semantics).
  Replace the validation with the inverted cross-var rule:
  ```hcl
  validation {
    condition     = var.enable_encryption || var.kms_key_arn == null
    error_message = "kms_key_arn must be null when enable_encryption is false."
  }
  ```
- `kms_key_arn`: description updated ("optional CMK; when null and encryption on, the module
  creates its own").

### `modules/s3-bucket/outputs.tf`
- `encryption_enabled`: `value = var.enable_encryption` (was `local.kms_enabled`; `local.kms_enabled`
  is removed).
- `kms_key_arn`: `value = local.effective_kms_key_arn` (effective key, not raw var).
- Add `created_dedicated_key` (bool) = `local.create_kms_key`.

### Root `variables.tf` / `outputs.tf`
- `enable_encryption` default `false` -> **`true`** (this is what makes trivy's root-default eval
  see a CMK). Description updated.
- `outputs.tf`: add `created_dedicated_key = module.bucket.created_dedicated_key`. `main.tf`
  wiring unchanged (root creates no key; the module does).

### Tests
- `tests/plan.tftest.hcl` - rewrite the 2 encryption runs, add a 3rd (assertions move from
  `length(aws_s3_bucket_server_side_encryption_configuration.this)` to `aws_kms_key.this`):
  - `creates_dedicated_key_when_none_provided`: defaults (enc on, no key) -> assert
    `length(aws_kms_key.this) == 1`.
  - `uses_provided_key`: `kms_key_arn="arn:...demo-key"` -> assert `length(aws_kms_key.this) == 0`
    and `...server_side_encryption_configuration.this.rule[0].apply_server_side_encryption_by_default[0].kms_master_key_id == "arn:...demo-key"` (exact block indexing verified by running the test).
  - `default_managed_key_when_encryption_disabled`: `enable_encryption=false` -> assert
    `length(aws_kms_key.this) == 0` and `kms_master_key_id == null`.
- `tests/validations.tftest.hcl` - rewrite `encryption_requires_kms_key` ->
  `key_forbidden_when_encryption_disabled`: `enable_encryption=false`, `kms_key_arn="arn:..."`,
  `expect_failures = [var.enable_encryption]`. Other 4 runs unchanged.
- `tests/deploy.tftest.hcl` - replace the `output.encryption_enabled == false` assertion
  (default is now CMK-on) with `output.created_dedicated_key == true` + `output.kms_key_arn != null`
  (real apply exercises module CMK creation end-to-end).
- `tests/parallel.tftest.hcl` - add `enable_encryption = false` to the file-level `variables`
  block (the test targets parallelism/state_key, not encryption; keeps it lean - no KMS key churn
  on the 2 parallel buckets). Assertions unchanged.

### `sweep.sh` (required by the chosen design - module now creates KMS keys that can leak)
- Broaden the tag query to S3 **and** KMS: `--resource-type-filters "s3" "kms"`.
- In the delete loop, branch on ARN: buckets -> empty+`delete-bucket` (existing); KMS ->
  `aws kms schedule-key-deletion --key-id <id> --pending-window-in-days 7` (keys cannot be
  hard-deleted). Update the header comment + dry-run listing. (No version header - this repo
  doesn't use the vault's script-versioning convention.)

## Verification (all local, no AWS creds needed for CI-green)
1. `terraform init` (root; module + setup resolved).
2. `terraform fmt -recursive -check` -> clean.
3. `tflint --recursive --config .tflint.hcl` -> exit 0.
4. `trivy config . --severity HIGH,CRITICAL --exit-code 1` -> **passes** (no AWS-0132).
5. `terraform test -filter=tests/validations.tftest.hcl -filter=tests/plan.tftest.hcl` ->
   creds-free (mock_provider) - confirms the rewritten assertions hold (key count, kms id
   propagation, validation expect_failures).
6. Apply tests (`deploy`, `parallel`) need `AWS_PROFILE=sandbox` (eu-west-1, SCP region-lock).
   Not run in CI (CI = validate+lint+scan only). Offer to run the full suite against the sandbox
   as a final confidence check before/after push.

## Land it
- Stage all changes (module, root, tests, sweep.sh) + the user's pending `~>` version / EN-comment
  edits in the working tree.
- **Amend** the single PR commit `b384bf0` (feature PR = one clean commit); tweak the body phrase
  "conditional SSE-KMS" -> "default customer-managed key encryption (CMK auto-created)" to stay
  accurate. Keep body lines <=100.
- `git push --force-with-lease`.
- Poll `gh pr checks` until `terraform-module-testing` is green (push re-triggers all checks).

## Out of scope (surfaced, not done)
- Root `providers.tf` still uses `>=` for aws/core. Per the same best practice it should be `~>`
  for a root. Left untouched (it's the demoed centerpiece, outside "fix what you added"); flag as
  a follow-up for the user to decide.

input.plan:
# Plan - Fix CI on PR #33 (terraform-module-testing)

## Context

PR #33 (`feat/terraform-module-testing`, repo `bgauduch/terraform-examples`) adds the live's
native `terraform test` example. CI had 3 red checks. Two are **already fixed and pushed**
(commit `b384bf0`, force-pushed):
- **Conventional commits** - commit body line was 120 chars; reflowed to <=100. GREEN.
- **terraform fmt / deferred-actions/02-after** - the latter was a transient 503 on trivy
  attestation download; the re-push re-ran it. GREEN.
- tflint findings in the example (missing `required_version`/provider version in
  `tests/setup/main.tf`, unused `enable_encryption`) - fixed. tflint now exits 0.
  Version constraints later corrected to root-style `~>` per HashiCorp best practice
  (root modules: `~>` lower+upper bound; reusable modules: `>=`). User also translated
  touched-file comments FR->EN.

**Remaining red check:** `validate + lint + scan (terraform-module-testing)` now fails at the
**trivy** step (it previously failed earlier at tflint, so trivy never ran). Single finding:

> **AWS-0132 (HIGH)** - `modules/s3-bucket/main.tf:7-11` - Bucket does not encrypt data with a
> customer managed key.

Root cause: the module makes SSE-KMS **conditional** (`count = kms_enabled ? 1 : 0`); with the
root's default vars (`enable_encryption=false`, `kms_key_arn=null`) the encryption resource is
absent, so trivy (static scan of the root default path) sees an unencrypted-by-CMK bucket and
gates the job (HIGH,CRITICAL -> exit 1). Confirmed locally with trivy 0.71.2. Only this one
finding (no whack-a-mole).

**Verified fact (both Explore agents):** AWS-0132 passes IFF the bucket has an always-attached
`aws_s3_bucket_server_side_encryption_configuration` with `sse_algorithm="aws:kms"` AND
`kms_master_key_id` resolving to a **non-null CMK** in the root's default eval. The sister
`deferred-actions/02-after` example passes because its root creates `aws_kms_key.root` and
always injects it.

## Decision (user-chosen)

Make the **module encrypt by default with a CMK** (secure-by-default at the module level, not
patched at the root), and **keep `enable_encryption`** (repurposed). The module **creates its
own CMK** when none is provided - the only way to satisfy AWS-0132 without the root supplying a
key.

New semantics:
- `enable_encryption` (default **true**) = "encrypt with a customer-managed key (CMK)".
  - `true` + `kms_key_arn` set -> use the provided CMK.
  - `true` + `kms_key_arn` null -> **module creates a dedicated `aws_kms_key`** and uses it.
  - `false` -> SSE-KMS with the **aws/s3 default managed key** (runtime path; trivy sees the
    default `true`, so the gate passes).
- The **conditional resource** (for the plan-assertion teaching point) moves from the encryption
  block to the **KMS key creation** (`aws_kms_key.this`, created only when no key is provided).
- Cross-variable validation + `expect_failures` demo is **preserved** by inverting the rule:
  `kms_key_arn` is forbidden when `enable_encryption=false`.

Comments in every touched file go **EN** (matches the user's FR->EN migration of this repo).

## Changes by file

### `modules/s3-bucket/main.tf` (core)

Replace the `locals` + the conditional encryption resource. Add a conditional KMS key.

```hcl
locals {
  # Encrypt by default with a customer-managed key (CMK). With no kms_key_arn the
  # module creates its own CMK so the bucket is never left on the aws/s3 default key.
  create_kms_key = var.enable_encryption && var.kms_key_arn == null
  # Effective key feeding the SSE rule: caller-provided, else module-created, else
  # null -> bucket uses S3's default managed key (aws/s3) when encryption is off.
  effective_kms_key_arn = var.enable_encryption ? coalesce(var.kms_key_arn, one(aws_kms_key.this[*].arn)) : null
}

resource "aws_kms_key" "this" {
  count                   = local.create_kms_key ? 1 : 0
  description             = "Module-managed CMK for bucket ${var.bucket_name}"
  enable_key_rotation     = true
  deletion_window_in_days = 7
}

# aws_s3_bucket / public_access_block / versioning: UNCHANGED

resource "aws_s3_bucket_server_side_encryption_configuration" "this" {
  # Always attached (secure-by-default); count removed.
  bucket = aws_s3_bucket.this.id
  rule {
    apply_server_side_encryption_by_default {
      sse_algorithm     = "aws:kms"
      kms_master_key_id = local.effective_kms_key_arn # CMK arn, or null -> aws/s3 key
    }
    bucket_key_enabled = true
  }
}
```
`coalesce` is never all-null (the only-true branch guarantees one non-null arg). `aws` provider
>=5.0 already declared in `versions.tf` supports `aws_kms_key` - no version bump.

### `modules/s3-bucket/variables.tf`
- `enable_encryption`: default `false` -> **`true`**; description rewritten (CMK semantics).
  Replace the validation with the inverted cross-var rule:
  ```hcl
  validation {
    condition     = var.enable_encryption || var.kms_key_arn == null
    error_message = "kms_key_arn must be null when enable_encryption is false."
  }
  ```
- `kms_key_arn`: description updated ("optional CMK; when null and encryption on, the module
  creates its own").

### `modules/s3-bucket/outputs.tf`
- `encryption_enabled`: `value = var.enable_encryption` (was `local.kms_enabled`; `local.kms_enabled`
  is removed).
- `kms_key_arn`: `value = local.effective_kms_key_arn` (effective key, not raw var).
- Add `created_dedicated_key` (bool) = `local.create_kms_key`.

### Root `variables.tf` / `outputs.tf`
- `enable_encryption` default `false` -> **`true`** (this is what makes trivy's root-default eval
  see a CMK). Description updated.
- `outputs.tf`: add `created_dedicated_key = module.bucket.created_dedicated_key`. `main.tf`
  wiring unchanged (root creates no key; the module does).

### Tests
- `tests/plan.tftest.hcl` - rewrite the 2 encryption runs, add a 3rd (assertions move from
  `length(aws_s3_bucket_server_side_encryption_configuration.this)` to `aws_kms_key.this`):
  - `creates_dedicated_key_when_none_provided`: defaults (enc on, no key) -> assert
    `length(aws_kms_key.this) == 1`.
  - `uses_provided_key`: `kms_key_arn="arn:...demo-key"` -> assert `length(aws_kms_key.this) == 0`
    and `...server_side_encryption_configuration.this.rule[0].apply_server_side_encryption_by_default[0].kms_master_key_id == "arn:...demo-key"` (exact block indexing verified by running the test).
  - `default_managed_key_when_encryption_disabled`: `enable_encryption=false` -> assert
    `length(aws_kms_key.this) == 0` and `kms_master_key_id == null`.
- `tests/validations.tftest.hcl` - rewrite `encryption_requires_kms_key` ->
  `key_forbidden_when_encryption_disabled`: `enable_encryption=false`, `kms_key_arn="arn:..."`,
  `expect_failures = [var.enable_encryption]`. Other 4 runs unchanged.
- `tests/deploy.tftest.hcl` - replace the `output.encryption_enabled == false` assertion
  (default is now CMK-on) with `output.created_dedicated_key == true` + `output.kms_key_arn != null`
  (real apply exercises module CMK creation end-to-end).
- `tests/parallel.tftest.hcl` - add `enable_encryption = false` to the file-level `variables`
  block (the test targets parallelism/state_key, not encryption; keeps it lean - no KMS key churn
  on the 2 parallel buckets). Assertions unchanged.

### `sweep.sh` (required by the chosen design - module now creates KMS keys that can leak)
- Broaden the tag query to S3 **and** KMS: `--resource-type-filters "s3" "kms"`.
- In the delete loop, branch on ARN: buckets -> empty+`delete-bucket` (existing); KMS ->
  `aws kms schedule-key-deletion --key-id <id> --pending-window-in-days 7` (keys cannot be
  hard-deleted). Update the header comment + dry-run listing. (No version header - this repo
  doesn't use the vault's script-versioning convention.)

## Verification (all local, no AWS creds needed for CI-green)
1. `terraform init` (root; module + setup resolved).
2. `terraform fmt -recursive -check` -> clean.
3. `tflint --recursive --config .tflint.hcl` -> exit 0.
4. `trivy config . --severity HIGH,CRITICAL --exit-code 1` -> **passes** (no AWS-0132).
5. `terraform test -filter=tests/validations.tftest.hcl -filter=tests/plan.tftest.hcl` ->
   creds-free (mock_provider) - confirms the rewritten assertions hold (key count, kms id
   propagation, validation expect_failures).
6. Apply tests (`deploy`, `parallel`) need `AWS_PROFILE=sandbox` (eu-west-1, SCP region-lock).
   Not run in CI (CI = validate+lint+scan only). Offer to run the full suite against the sandbox
   as a final confidence check before/after push.

## Land it
- Stage all changes (module, root, tests, sweep.sh) + the user's pending `~>` version / EN-comment
  edits in the working tree.
- **Amend** the single PR commit `b384bf0` (feature PR = one clean commit); tweak the body phrase
  "conditional SSE-KMS" -> "default customer-managed key encryption (CMK auto-created)" to stay
  accurate. Keep body lines <=100.
- `git push --force-with-lease`.
- Poll `gh pr checks` until `terraform-module-testing` is green (push re-triggers all checks).

## Out of scope (surfaced, not done)
- Root `providers.tf` still uses `>=` for aws/core. Per the same best practice it should be `~>`
  for a root. Left untouched (it's the demoed centerpiece, outside "fix what you added"); flag as
  a follow-up for the user to decide.

content.text:
Définitif. Filtrons proprement - mais d'abord le résultat clé est déjà là:

**AWS-0132 verdict empirique:**
| Variante | sse_algorithm | kms_master_key_id | AWS-0132 |
|---|---|---|---|
| v1 SSE-S3 | `AES256` | (clé gérée S3) | ❌ **FAIL** |
| v2 KMS managée | `aws:kms` | null (clé `aws/s3`) | ❌ **FAIL** |
| v3 CMK | `aws:kms` | vrai ARN | ✅ PASS |
| v4 aucune | - | - | ❌ FAIL |

Donc une **clé gérée ne passe pas** le gate - il faut une vraie CMK. Pourquoi deferred-actions passe alors ? Parce que son **root crée `aws_kms_key.root` et l'injecte** - ce n'est pas le module qui crée la clé, c'est le root. Tu as raison sur le fond: pas besoin de créer la clé dans le module. On peut mettre la CMK au **root** (pattern deferred-actions exact) et **laisser le module + les tests plan/validation inchangés**. Je vérifie que ce pattern passe trivy avec une clé conditionnelle (référence de ressource, pas littéral).

input.content:
# Plan - Fix CI on PR #33 (terraform-module-testing) - A1 (root provides CMK)

## Context

PR #33 (`feat/terraform-module-testing`, `bgauduch/terraform-examples`) adds the live's native
`terraform test` example. CI had 3 red checks; two are **already fixed and pushed** (commit
`b384bf0`):
- **Conventional commits**: 120-char body line reflowed to <=100. GREEN.
- **fmt / deferred-actions/02-after**: the latter was a transient 503 on trivy attestation; the
  re-push re-ran it. GREEN. tflint findings in the example (missing `required_version`/provider
  version in `tests/setup/main.tf`; `enable_encryption` unused) also fixed; version constraints
  set to root-style `~>` per HashiCorp best practice. User translated touched-file comments FR->EN.

**Remaining red check:** `validate + lint + scan (terraform-module-testing)` now fails at the
**trivy** step (it previously died earlier at tflint, so trivy never ran). Single finding:
**AWS-0132 (HIGH)** - the example bucket isn't encrypted with a customer-managed key (CMK).

## Empirical findings (probed locally, trivy 0.71.2)

Settles the design. AWS-0132 verdict per encryption style:

| Encryption style | sse_algorithm | kms_master_key_id | AWS-0132 |
|---|---|---|---|
| SSE-S3 | `AES256` | (S3-managed) | **FAIL** |
| KMS managed | `aws:kms` | null (`aws/s3` key) | **FAIL** |
| **CMK** | `aws:kms` | real key ARN | **PASS** |
| none | - | - | FAIL |

=> A managed key does NOT satisfy the gate. A real CMK does. `deferred-actions/02-after` passes
**only because its root creates `aws_kms_key.root` and injects it** - the module does not self-create.

Second probe: with a **root-provided CMK reference**, BOTH a conditional-`count` SSE config
(module as-is) AND an always-on SSE config PASS AWS-0132. So we do NOT need to touch the module
or rewrite tests - we only need the **root** to provision and pass a CMK.

## Decision (supersedes the earlier "module creates its own CMK" idea)

**A1 - the root provisions a CMK and passes it to the module** (mirrors `deferred-actions`, gives
live-series continuity). The module's conditional SSE-KMS, the cross-variable validations, and the
plan/validation tests stay **unchanged**. Minimal blast radius.

## Changes

### `examples/terraform-module-testing/main.tf` (root)
Add a KMS key and wire it into the module call (replacing the var-driven encryption inputs):
```hcl
# The example bucket is encrypted with a customer-managed key by default. The root
# provisions the CMK and passes it down (same pattern as the deferred-actions live).
resource "aws_kms_key" "example" {
  description             = "CMK for the terraform-module-testing example bucket"
  enable_key_rotation     = true
  deletion_window_in_days = 7
}

module "bucket" {
  source             = "./modules/s3-bucket"
  bucket_name        = var.bucket_name
  environment        = var.environment
  enable_encryption  = true
  kms_key_arn        = aws_kms_key.example.arn
  force_destroy      = var.force_destroy
  versioning_enabled = var.versioning_enabled
  tags               = { example = "terraform-module-testing" }
}
```

### `examples/terraform-module-testing/variables.tf` (root)
`enable_encryption` and `kms_key_arn` are no longer referenced at the root (hardcoded above) ->
**remove both** to avoid tflint `terraform_unused_declarations`. No test sets them at root level
(deploy/parallel only set bucket_name/environment/force_destroy/versioning_enabled), so this is safe.
(`run_id`, `region`, `bucket_name`, `environment`, `force_destroy`, `versioning_enabled` stay.)

### `examples/terraform-module-testing/outputs.tf` (root)
Unchanged - `encryption_enabled` / `kms_key_arn` outputs map to the module outputs (not the removed
root vars).

### `examples/terraform-module-testing/tests/deploy.tftest.hcl`
The default is now CMK-on, so flip the one encryption assertion in `deploy_and_assert`:
```hcl
assert {
  condition     = output.encryption_enabled == true
  error_message = "the example bucket must be encrypted with a CMK by default"
}
assert {
  condition     = output.kms_key_arn != null
  error_message = "a CMK ARN must be exposed for the deployed bucket"
}
```

### `examples/terraform-module-testing/sweep.sh`
The root now creates a KMS key on every real apply (`deploy`, `parallel`), which can leak if a test
is interrupted before auto-destroy. Extend the sweeper (today S3-only):
- Broaden the tag query: `--resource-type-filters "s3" "kms"`.
- In the delete loop, branch on ARN: bucket -> empty + `delete-bucket` (existing); KMS ->
  `aws kms schedule-key-deletion --key-id <id> --pending-window-in-days 7` (keys can't be hard-deleted).
- Update header comment + dry-run listing. (No version header - this repo doesn't use the vault's
  script-versioning convention.)

### Untouched (key point)
`modules/s3-bucket/**` (conditional `count = kms_enabled ? 1 : 0` kept), `tests/plan.tftest.hcl`,
`tests/validations.tftest.hcl`, `tests/parallel.tftest.hcl`. The user's pending working-tree edits
(`~>` version constraints, EN comments) are kept.

## Verification (local, no AWS creds needed for CI-green)
1. `terraform init`
2. `terraform fmt -recursive -check` -> clean
3. `tflint --recursive --config .tflint.hcl` -> exit 0 (confirms no unused-var after removing root vars)
4. `trivy config . --severity HIGH,CRITICAL --exit-code 1` -> **passes** (no AWS-0132)
5. `terraform test -filter=tests/validations.tftest.hcl -filter=tests/plan.tftest.hcl` -> creds-free
   (mock_provider); confirms module + those tests still green
6. Apply tests (`deploy`, `parallel`) need `AWS_PROFILE=sandbox` (eu-west-1). Not run in CI. Offer to
   run the full suite against the sandbox as a final confidence check.

## Land it
- Stage the changed files + pending working-tree edits.
- **Amend** the single PR commit `b384bf0` (feature PR = one clean commit). Body stays accurate
  ("conditional SSE-KMS" still true) - no body change. Lines already <=100.
- `git push --force-with-lease`; poll `gh pr checks` until `terraform-module-testing` is green.

## Out of scope (surfaced, not done)
- Root `providers.tf` still uses `>=` for aws/core; per the same best practice a root should use `~>`.
  Left untouched (demoed centerpiece, outside "fix what you added"); flag as a follow-up.

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Plan - Fix CI on PR #33 (terraform-module-testing)

## Context

PR #33 (`feat/terraform-module-testing`, repo `bgauduch/terraform-examples`) adds the live's
native `terraform test` example. CI had 3 red checks. Two are **already fixed and pushed**
(commit `b384bf0`, force-pushed):
- **Conventional commits** - commit body line was 120 chars; reflowed to <=100. GREEN.
- **terraform fmt / deferred-actions/02-after** - the latter was a transient 503 on trivy
  attestation download; the re-push re-ran it. GREEN.
- tflint findings in the example (missing `required_version`/provider version in
  `tests/setup/main.tf`, unused `enable_encryption`) - fixed. tflint now exits 0.
  Version constraints later corrected to root-style `~>` per HashiCorp best practice
  (root modules: `~>` lower+upper bound; reusable modules: `>=`). User also translated
  touched-file comments FR->EN.

**Remaining red check:** `validate + lint + scan (terraform-module-testing)` now fails at the
**trivy** step (it previously failed earlier at tflint, so trivy never ran). Single finding:

> **AWS-0132 (HIGH)** - `modules/s3-bucket/main.tf:7-11` - Bucket does not encrypt data with a
> customer managed key.

Root cause: the module makes SSE-KMS **conditional** (`count = kms_enabled ? 1 : 0`); with the
root's default vars (`enable_encryption=false`, `kms_key_arn=null`) the encryption resource is
absent, so trivy (static scan of the root default path) sees an unencrypted-by-CMK bucket and
gates the job (HIGH,CRITICAL -> exit 1). Confirmed locally with trivy 0.71.2. Only this one
finding (no whack-a-mole).

**Verified fact (both Explore agents):** AWS-0132 passes IFF the bucket has an always-attached
`aws_s3_bucket_server_side_encryption_configuration` with `sse_algorithm="aws:kms"` AND
`kms_master_key_id` resolving to a **non-null CMK** in the root's default eval. The sister
`deferred-actions/02-after` example passes because its root creates `aws_kms_key.root` and
always injects it.

## Decision (user-chosen)

Make the **module encrypt by default with a CMK** (secure-by-default at the module level, not
patched at the root), and **keep `enable_encryption`** (repurposed). The module **creates its
own CMK** when none is provided - the only way to satisfy AWS-0132 without the root supplying a
key.

New semantics:
- `enable_encryption` (default **true**) = "encrypt with a customer-managed key (CMK)".
  - `true` + `kms_key_arn` set -> use the provided CMK.
  - `true` + `kms_key_arn` null -> **module creates a dedicated `aws_kms_key`** and uses it.
  - `false` -> SSE-KMS with the **aws/s3 default managed key** (runtime path; trivy sees the
    default `true`, so the gate passes).
- The **conditional resource** (for the plan-assertion teaching point) moves from the encryption
  block to the **KMS key creation** (`aws_kms_key.this`, created only when no key is provided).
- Cross-variable validation + `expect_failures` demo is **preserved** by inverting the rule:
  `kms_key_arn` is forbidden when `enable_encryption=false`.

Comments in every touched file go **EN** (matches the user's FR->EN migration of this repo).

## Changes by file

### `modules/s3-bucket/main.tf` (core)

Replace the `locals` + the conditional encryption resource. Add a conditional KMS key.

```hcl
locals {
  # Encrypt by default with a customer-managed key (CMK). With no kms_key_arn the
  # module creates its own CMK so the bucket is never left on the aws/s3 default key.
  create_kms_key = var.enable_encryption && var.kms_key_arn == null
  # Effective key feeding the SSE rule: caller-provided, else module-created, else
  # null -> bucket uses S3's default managed key (aws/s3) when encryption is off.
  effective_kms_key_arn = var.enable_encryption ? coalesce(var.kms_key_arn, one(aws_kms_key.this[*].arn)) : null
}

resource "aws_kms_key" "this" {
  count                   = local.create_kms_key ? 1 : 0
  description             = "Module-managed CMK for bucket ${var.bucket_name}"
  enable_key_rotation     = true
  deletion_window_in_days = 7
}

# aws_s3_bucket / public_access_block / versioning: UNCHANGED

resource "aws_s3_bucket_server_side_encryption_configuration" "this" {
  # Always attached (secure-by-default); count removed.
  bucket = aws_s3_bucket.this.id
  rule {
    apply_server_side_encryption_by_default {
      sse_algorithm     = "aws:kms"
      kms_master_key_id = local.effective_kms_key_arn # CMK arn, or null -> aws/s3 key
    }
    bucket_key_enabled = true
  }
}
```
`coalesce` is never all-null (the only-true branch guarantees one non-null arg). `aws` provider
>=5.0 already declared in `versions.tf` supports `aws_kms_key` - no version bump.

### `modules/s3-bucket/variables.tf`
- `enable_encryption`: default `false` -> **`true`**; description rewritten (CMK semantics).
  Replace the validation with the inverted cross-var rule:
  ```hcl
  validation {
    condition     = var.enable_encryption || var.kms_key_arn == null
    error_message = "kms_key_arn must be null when enable_encryption is false."
  }
  ```
- `kms_key_arn`: description updated ("optional CMK; when null and encryption on, the module
  creates its own").

### `modules/s3-bucket/outputs.tf`
- `encryption_enabled`: `value = var.enable_encryption` (was `local.kms_enabled`; `local.kms_enabled`
  is removed).
- `kms_key_arn`: `value = local.effective_kms_key_arn` (effective key, not raw var).
- Add `created_dedicated_key` (bool) = `local.create_kms_key`.

### Root `variables.tf` / `outputs.tf`
- `enable_encryption` default `false` -> **`true`** (this is what makes trivy's root-default eval
  see a CMK). Description updated.
- `outputs.tf`: add `created_dedicated_key = module.bucket.created_dedicated_key`. `main.tf`
  wiring unchanged (root creates no key; the module does).

### Tests
- `tests/plan.tftest.hcl` - rewrite the 2 encryption runs, add a 3rd (assertions move from
  `length(aws_s3_bucket_server_side_encryption_configuration.this)` to `aws_kms_key.this`):
  - `creates_dedicated_key_when_none_provided`: defaults (enc on, no key) -> assert
    `length(aws_kms_key.this) == 1`.
  - `uses_provided_key`: `kms_key_arn="arn:...demo-key"` -> assert `length(aws_kms_key.this) == 0`
    and `...server_side_encryption_configuration.this.rule[0].apply_server_side_encryption_by_default[0].kms_master_key_id == "arn:...demo-key"` (exact block indexing verified by running the test).
  - `default_managed_key_when_encryption_disabled`: `enable_encryption=false` -> assert
    `length(aws_kms_key.this) == 0` and `kms_master_key_id == null`.
- `tests/validations.tftest.hcl` - rewrite `encryption_requires_kms_key` ->
  `key_forbidden_when_encryption_disabled`: `enable_encryption=false`, `kms_key_arn="arn:..."`,
  `expect_failures = [var.enable_encryption]`. Other 4 runs unchanged.
- `tests/deploy.tftest.hcl` - replace the `output.encryption_enabled == false` assertion
  (default is now CMK-on) with `output.created_dedicated_key == true` + `output.kms_key_arn != null`
  (real apply exercises module CMK creation end-to-end).
- `tests/parallel.tftest.hcl` - add `enable_encryption = false` to the file-level `variables`
  block (the test targets parallelism/state_key, not encryption; keeps it lean - no KMS key churn
  on the 2 parallel buckets). Assertions unchanged.

### `sweep.sh` (required by the chosen design - module now creates KMS keys that can leak)
- Broaden the tag query to S3 **and** KMS: `--resource-type-filters "s3" "kms"`.
- In the delete loop, branch on ARN: buckets -> empty+`delete-bucket` (existing); KMS ->
  `aws kms schedule-key-deletion --key-id <id> --pending-window-in-days 7` (keys cannot be
  hard-deleted). Update the header comment + dry-run listing. (No version header - this repo
  doesn't use the vault's script-versioning convention.)

## Verification (all local, no AWS creds needed for CI-green)
1. `terraform init` (root; module + setup resolved).
2. `terraform fmt -recursive -check` -> clean.
3. `tflint --recursive --config .tflint.hcl` -> exit 0.
4. `trivy config . --severity HIGH,CRITICAL --exit-code 1` -> **passes** (no AWS-0132).
5. `terraform test -filter=tests/validations.tftest.hcl -filter=tests/plan.tftest.hcl` ->
   creds-free (mock_provider) - confirms the rewritten assertions hold (key count, kms id
   propagation, validation expect_failures).
6. Apply tests (`deploy`, `parallel`) need `AWS_PROFILE=sandbox` (eu-west-1, SCP region-lock).
   Not run in CI (CI = validate+lint+scan only). Offer to run the full suite against the sandbox
   as a final confidence check before/after push.

## Land it
- Stage all changes (module, root, tests, sweep.sh) + the user's pending `~>` version / EN-comment
  edits in the working tree.
- **Amend** the single PR commit `b384bf0` (feature PR = one clean commit); tweak the body phrase
  "conditional SSE-KMS" -> "default customer-managed key encryption (CMK auto-created)" to stay
  accurate. Keep body lines <=100.
- `git push --force-with-lease`.
- Poll `gh pr checks` until `terraform-module-testing` is green (push re-triggers all checks).

## Out of scope (surfaced, not done)
- Root `providers.tf` still uses `>=` for aws/core. Per the same best practice it should be `~>`
  for a root. Left untouched (it's the demoed centerpiece, outside "fix what you added"); flag as
  a follow-up for the user to decide.

input.plan:
# Plan - Fix CI on PR #33 (terraform-module-testing) - A1 (root provides CMK)

## Context

PR #33 (`feat/terraform-module-testing`, `bgauduch/terraform-examples`) adds the live's native
`terraform test` example. CI had 3 red checks; two are **already fixed and pushed** (commit
`b384bf0`):
- **Conventional commits**: 120-char body line reflowed to <=100. GREEN.
- **fmt / deferred-actions/02-after**: the latter was a transient 503 on trivy attestation; the
  re-push re-ran it. GREEN. tflint findings in the example (missing `required_version`/provider
  version in `tests/setup/main.tf`; `enable_encryption` unused) also fixed; version constraints
  set to root-style `~>` per HashiCorp best practice. User translated touched-file comments FR->EN.

**Remaining red check:** `validate + lint + scan (terraform-module-testing)` now fails at the
**trivy** step (it previously died earlier at tflint, so trivy never ran). Single finding:
**AWS-0132 (HIGH)** - the example bucket isn't encrypted with a customer-managed key (CMK).

## Empirical findings (probed locally, trivy 0.71.2)

Settles the design. AWS-0132 verdict per encryption style:

| Encryption style | sse_algorithm | kms_master_key_id | AWS-0132 |
|---|---|---|---|
| SSE-S3 | `AES256` | (S3-managed) | **FAIL** |
| KMS managed | `aws:kms` | null (`aws/s3` key) | **FAIL** |
| **CMK** | `aws:kms` | real key ARN | **PASS** |
| none | - | - | FAIL |

=> A managed key does NOT satisfy the gate. A real CMK does. `deferred-actions/02-after` passes
**only because its root creates `aws_kms_key.root` and injects it** - the module does not self-create.

Second probe: with a **root-provided CMK reference**, BOTH a conditional-`count` SSE config
(module as-is) AND an always-on SSE config PASS AWS-0132. So we do NOT need to touch the module
or rewrite tests - we only need the **root** to provision and pass a CMK.

## Decision (supersedes the earlier "module creates its own CMK" idea)

**A1 - the root provisions a CMK and passes it to the module** (mirrors `deferred-actions`, gives
live-series continuity). The module's conditional SSE-KMS, the cross-variable validations, and the
plan/validation tests stay **unchanged**. Minimal blast radius.

## Changes

### `examples/terraform-module-testing/main.tf` (root)
Add a KMS key and wire it into the module call (replacing the var-driven encryption inputs):
```hcl
# The example bucket is encrypted with a customer-managed key by default. The root
# provisions the CMK and passes it down (same pattern as the deferred-actions live).
resource "aws_kms_key" "example" {
  description             = "CMK for the terraform-module-testing example bucket"
  enable_key_rotation     = true
  deletion_window_in_days = 7
}

module "bucket" {
  source             = "./modules/s3-bucket"
  bucket_name        = var.bucket_name
  environment        = var.environment
  enable_encryption  = true
  kms_key_arn        = aws_kms_key.example.arn
  force_destroy      = var.force_destroy
  versioning_enabled = var.versioning_enabled
  tags               = { example = "terraform-module-testing" }
}
```

### `examples/terraform-module-testing/variables.tf` (root)
`enable_encryption` and `kms_key_arn` are no longer referenced at the root (hardcoded above) ->
**remove both** to avoid tflint `terraform_unused_declarations`. No test sets them at root level
(deploy/parallel only set bucket_name/environment/force_destroy/versioning_enabled), so this is safe.
(`run_id`, `region`, `bucket_name`, `environment`, `force_destroy`, `versioning_enabled` stay.)

### `examples/terraform-module-testing/outputs.tf` (root)
Unchanged - `encryption_enabled` / `kms_key_arn` outputs map to the module outputs (not the removed
root vars).

### `examples/terraform-module-testing/tests/deploy.tftest.hcl`
The default is now CMK-on, so flip the one encryption assertion in `deploy_and_assert`:
```hcl
assert {
  condition     = output.encryption_enabled == true
  error_message = "the example bucket must be encrypted with a CMK by default"
}
assert {
  condition     = output.kms_key_arn != null
  error_message = "a CMK ARN must be exposed for the deployed bucket"
}
```

### `examples/terraform-module-testing/sweep.sh`
The root now creates a KMS key on every real apply (`deploy`, `parallel`), which can leak if a test
is interrupted before auto-destroy. Extend the sweeper (today S3-only):
- Broaden the tag query: `--resource-type-filters "s3" "kms"`.
- In the delete loop, branch on ARN: bucket -> empty + `delete-bucket` (existing); KMS ->
  `aws kms schedule-key-deletion --key-id <id> --pending-window-in-days 7` (keys can't be hard-deleted).
- Update header comment + dry-run listing. (No version header - this repo doesn't use the vault's
  script-versioning convention.)

### Untouched (key point)
`modules/s3-bucket/**` (conditional `count = kms_enabled ? 1 : 0` kept), `tests/plan.tftest.hcl`,
`tests/validations.tftest.hcl`, `tests/parallel.tftest.hcl`. The user's pending working-tree edits
(`~>` version constraints, EN comments) are kept.

## Verification (local, no AWS creds needed for CI-green)
1. `terraform init`
2. `terraform fmt -recursive -check` -> clean
3. `tflint --recursive --config .tflint.hcl` -> exit 0 (confirms no unused-var after removing root vars)
4. `trivy config . --severity HIGH,CRITICAL --exit-code 1` -> **passes** (no AWS-0132)
5. `terraform test -filter=tests/validations.tftest.hcl -filter=tests/plan.tftest.hcl` -> creds-free
   (mock_provider); confirms module + those tests still green
6. Apply tests (`deploy`, `parallel`) need `AWS_PROFILE=sandbox` (eu-west-1). Not run in CI. Offer to
   run the full suite against the sandbox as a final confidence check.

## Land it
- Stage the changed files + pending working-tree edits.
- **Amend** the single PR commit `b384bf0` (feature PR = one clean commit). Body stays accurate
  ("conditional SSE-KMS" still true) - no body change. Lines already <=100.
- `git push --force-with-lease`; poll `gh pr checks` until `terraform-module-testing` is green.

## Out of scope (surfaced, not done)
- Root `providers.tf` still uses `>=` for aws/core; per the same best practice a root should use `~>`.
  Left untouched (demoed centerpiece, outside "fix what you added"); flag as a follow-up.

(B) CONVENTION DE TITRE des lives : titre = `❯ live tech_ · <sujet>`, documentée en §5.5 (sibling textuel de la marque), add-editorial-piece (format live) préfixe auto, note Éditorial/CLAUDE.md + commentaire template piece. (C) ADR commun (décision structurante) + check lint (squelettes portent le bloc canonique ; titres format:live conformes) + bump design-system version + journal README brand. content.text:
Compris - on reste pragmatique pour l'exemple:
- module **toujours chiffré** (CMK si fournie, sinon clé AWS-managée S3),
- **exception trivy** AWS-0132 + commentaire "durcir plus tard (CMK)",
- garder l'amélioration A1 pour plus tard,
- **rien casser** côté tests/démo.

Avant de figer ça, je dois vérifier un point critique: la CI fait `trivy config -o json` **puis** `trivy convert --exit-code 1`. Je teste qu'un `#trivy:ignore` inline survit bien à cette 2-step (sinon l'exception ne passera pas le gate).

content.text:
**Mécanisme d'ignore prouvé** (A/B propre sur une finding qui se déclenche vraiment):
- sans ignore: AWS-0132 présent dans le JSON, **gate exit=1**
- avec `#trivy:ignore:AWS-0132` sur la ressource SSE config: finding retirée du JSON, **gate exit=0**

L'ignore inline survit au `config -o json` → `convert --exit-code 1`. Placement = sur la ressource `aws_s3_bucket_server_side_encryption_configuration` (là où trivy attribue la finding quand le bloc existe). J'ai tout ce qu'il faut. Je réécris le plan en version minimale.

input.content:
# Plan - Fix CI on PR #33 - always-encrypt + documented trivy exception

## Context

PR #33 (`bgauduch/terraform-examples`, branch `feat/terraform-module-testing`) adds the live's
`terraform test` example. Two of three red checks already fixed + pushed (commit `b384bf0`):
commitlint (body reflow), fmt, deferred-actions/02 (transient 503), and the example's tflint
findings (`tests/setup` version constraints -> root-style `~>`; `enable_encryption` now used).

**Remaining red check:** `validate + lint + scan (terraform-module-testing)` fails at **trivy**:
single finding **AWS-0132 (HIGH)** - the example bucket isn't encrypted with a customer-managed key
(the module makes SSE-KMS conditional; with default vars there's no encryption at all).

## Empirical validation (trivy 0.71.2, probed locally)
- A managed key does NOT satisfy AWS-0132: `AES256` (SSE-S3) FAILS, `aws:kms` with no key id
  (aws/s3 managed key) FAILS; only `aws:kms` + a real CMK ARN PASSES.
- An inline `#trivy:ignore:AWS-0132` on the `aws_s3_bucket_server_side_encryption_configuration`
  resource **survives the CI 2-step** (`trivy config -o json` then `trivy convert --exit-code 1`):
  A/B proven - without it gate exit=1, with it the finding is dropped from the JSON and gate exit=0.

## Decision (per user)
Pragmatic for the example - don't over-engineer:
1. **Always encrypt the bucket.** With a CMK (when `enable_encryption` + `kms_key_arn`) -> SSE-KMS
   with that key; otherwise fall back to the **AWS-managed key for S3** (`kms_master_key_id = null`).
   This is strictly better than today (today: no encryption when no key).
2. **Add a documented trivy exception** (`#trivy:ignore:AWS-0132`) - the fallback path uses a
   managed key, not a CMK, which AWS-0132 flags.
3. **Leave a `TODO(security)` comment** to harden later (require/provision a CMK, e.g. the
   "root provisions a CMK" pattern - kept as a future improvement, not done now).
4. **Break nothing else** - verify all examples + the demo are unaffected.

## Changes (minimal)

### `examples/terraform-module-testing/modules/s3-bucket/main.tf`
Make the SSE config always-on + add the exception. `local.kms_enabled` is kept (drives the CMK
choice + the `encryption_enabled` output); `enable_encryption`, its cross-variable validation, and
all other resources stay as-is.
```hcl
# The bucket is always encrypted. With a CMK (enable_encryption + kms_key_arn) it uses
# SSE-KMS with that key; otherwise it falls back to the AWS-managed key for S3
# (kms_master_key_id = null). trivy AWS-0132 wants a customer-managed key, which the
# fallback path doesn't use, so it is ignored here.
# TODO(security): require a CMK to satisfy AWS-0132 for real - e.g. provision one in the
# root and inject it (like the deferred-actions example). Deferred hardening.
#trivy:ignore:AWS-0132
resource "aws_s3_bucket_server_side_encryption_configuration" "this" {
  bucket = aws_s3_bucket.this.id
  rule {
    apply_server_side_encryption_by_default {
      sse_algorithm     = "aws:kms"
      kms_master_key_id = local.kms_enabled ? var.kms_key_arn : null
    }
    bucket_key_enabled = true
  }
}
```
(was `count = local.kms_enabled ? 1 : 0` + `kms_master_key_id = var.kms_key_arn`)

### `examples/terraform-module-testing/tests/plan.tftest.hcl`
The SSE resource is no longer count-indexed, so the two encryption runs change from
presence/absence to key-id value (exact `rule` indexing verified by running the test):
- `no_encryption_block_without_key` -> `falls_back_to_managed_key_without_cmk`
  (`enable_encryption=false`): assert
  `one(aws_s3_bucket_server_side_encryption_configuration.this.rule).apply_server_side_encryption_by_default[0].kms_master_key_id == null`.
- `encryption_block_present_with_key` -> `uses_provided_cmk` (`enable_encryption=true`,
  `kms_key_arn="arn:...demo-key"`): assert the same path `== "arn:...demo-key"` (drop the
  `length(...) == 1` assertion).

### `examples/terraform-module-testing/tests/deploy.tftest.hcl`
`output.encryption_enabled == false` still holds (no CMK in use by default). Only update the
`error_message` wording to "CMK encryption must be off (managed-key fallback) ..." for accuracy.

### `examples/terraform-module-testing/modules/s3-bucket/outputs.tf`
Clarify the `encryption_enabled` description ("true when a customer-managed key is in use").

### Untouched
Module `variables.tf` (incl. the `enable_encryption` cross-var validation),
`tests/validations.tftest.hcl`, `tests/parallel.tftest.hcl`, root `main.tf`/`variables.tf`/
`outputs.tf`, `sweep.sh` (no real CMK is created anywhere -> no KMS leak -> sweeper stays S3-only).
User's pending working-tree edits (`~>` constraints, EN comments) kept.

## Verification (local)
1. `terraform init`; `terraform fmt -recursive -check` -> clean.
2. `tflint --recursive --config .tflint.hcl` -> exit 0.
3. CI 2-step: `trivy config . -f json -o trivy.json --exit-code 0` then
   `trivy convert --severity HIGH,CRITICAL --exit-code 1 trivy.json` -> **exit 0** (AWS-0132 ignored,
   no other HIGH).
4. `terraform test -filter=tests/validations.tftest.hcl -filter=tests/plan.tftest.hcl` -> creds-free
   (mock_provider); confirms the rewritten assertions hold and validations still pass.
5. Apply tests (`deploy`, `parallel`) need `AWS_PROFILE=sandbox` (eu-west-1); not in CI. Offer to run
   the full suite against the sandbox as a final check.

## Land it
- Stage changes + pending working-tree edits; **amend** the single PR commit `b384bf0` (body still
  accurate, lines <=100); `git push --force-with-lease`; poll `gh pr checks` until green.

## Out of scope (surfaced)
- Root `providers.tf` still `>=` for aws/core (best practice: `~>` for a root) - follow-up.
- The "root provisions a CMK" hardening (A1) - the proper AWS-0132 fix - deferred per the TODO.

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Plan - Fix CI on PR #33 (terraform-module-testing) - A1 (root provides CMK)

## Context

PR #33 (`feat/terraform-module-testing`, `bgauduch/terraform-examples`) adds the live's native
`terraform test` example. CI had 3 red checks; two are **already fixed and pushed** (commit
`b384bf0`):
- **Conventional commits**: 120-char body line reflowed to <=100. GREEN.
- **fmt / deferred-actions/02-after**: the latter was a transient 503 on trivy attestation; the
  re-push re-ran it. GREEN. tflint findings in the example (missing `required_version`/provider
  version in `tests/setup/main.tf`; `enable_encryption` unused) also fixed; version constraints
  set to root-style `~>` per HashiCorp best practice. User translated touched-file comments FR->EN.

**Remaining red check:** `validate + lint + scan (terraform-module-testing)` now fails at the
**trivy** step (it previously died earlier at tflint, so trivy never ran). Single finding:
**AWS-0132 (HIGH)** - the example bucket isn't encrypted with a customer-managed key (CMK).

## Empirical findings (probed locally, trivy 0.71.2)

Settles the design. AWS-0132 verdict per encryption style:

| Encryption style | sse_algorithm | kms_master_key_id | AWS-0132 |
|---|---|---|---|
| SSE-S3 | `AES256` | (S3-managed) | **FAIL** |
| KMS managed | `aws:kms` | null (`aws/s3` key) | **FAIL** |
| **CMK** | `aws:kms` | real key ARN | **PASS** |
| none | - | - | FAIL |

=> A managed key does NOT satisfy the gate. A real CMK does. `deferred-actions/02-after` passes
**only because its root creates `aws_kms_key.root` and injects it** - the module does not self-create.

Second probe: with a **root-provided CMK reference**, BOTH a conditional-`count` SSE config
(module as-is) AND an always-on SSE config PASS AWS-0132. So we do NOT need to touch the module
or rewrite tests - we only need the **root** to provision and pass a CMK.

## Decision (supersedes the earlier "module creates its own CMK" idea)

**A1 - the root provisions a CMK and passes it to the module** (mirrors `deferred-actions`, gives
live-series continuity). The module's conditional SSE-KMS, the cross-variable validations, and the
plan/validation tests stay **unchanged**. Minimal blast radius.

## Changes

### `examples/terraform-module-testing/main.tf` (root)
Add a KMS key and wire it into the module call (replacing the var-driven encryption inputs):
```hcl
# The example bucket is encrypted with a customer-managed key by default. The root
# provisions the CMK and passes it down (same pattern as the deferred-actions live).
resource "aws_kms_key" "example" {
  description             = "CMK for the terraform-module-testing example bucket"
  enable_key_rotation     = true
  deletion_window_in_days = 7
}

module "bucket" {
  source             = "./modules/s3-bucket"
  bucket_name        = var.bucket_name
  environment        = var.environment
  enable_encryption  = true
  kms_key_arn        = aws_kms_key.example.arn
  force_destroy      = var.force_destroy
  versioning_enabled = var.versioning_enabled
  tags               = { example = "terraform-module-testing" }
}
```

### `examples/terraform-module-testing/variables.tf` (root)
`enable_encryption` and `kms_key_arn` are no longer referenced at the root (hardcoded above) ->
**remove both** to avoid tflint `terraform_unused_declarations`. No test sets them at root level
(deploy/parallel only set bucket_name/environment/force_destroy/versioning_enabled), so this is safe.
(`run_id`, `region`, `bucket_name`, `environment`, `force_destroy`, `versioning_enabled` stay.)

### `examples/terraform-module-testing/outputs.tf` (root)
Unchanged - `encryption_enabled` / `kms_key_arn` outputs map to the module outputs (not the removed
root vars).

### `examples/terraform-module-testing/tests/deploy.tftest.hcl`
The default is now CMK-on, so flip the one encryption assertion in `deploy_and_assert`:
```hcl
assert {
  condition     = output.encryption_enabled == true
  error_message = "the example bucket must be encrypted with a CMK by default"
}
assert {
  condition     = output.kms_key_arn != null
  error_message = "a CMK ARN must be exposed for the deployed bucket"
}
```

### `examples/terraform-module-testing/sweep.sh`
The root now creates a KMS key on every real apply (`deploy`, `parallel`), which can leak if a test
is interrupted before auto-destroy. Extend the sweeper (today S3-only):
- Broaden the tag query: `--resource-type-filters "s3" "kms"`.
- In the delete loop, branch on ARN: bucket -> empty + `delete-bucket` (existing); KMS ->
  `aws kms schedule-key-deletion --key-id <id> --pending-window-in-days 7` (keys can't be hard-deleted).
- Update header comment + dry-run listing. (No version header - this repo doesn't use the vault's
  script-versioning convention.)

### Untouched (key point)
`modules/s3-bucket/**` (conditional `count = kms_enabled ? 1 : 0` kept), `tests/plan.tftest.hcl`,
`tests/validations.tftest.hcl`, `tests/parallel.tftest.hcl`. The user's pending working-tree edits
(`~>` version constraints, EN comments) are kept.

## Verification (local, no AWS creds needed for CI-green)
1. `terraform init`
2. `terraform fmt -recursive -check` -> clean
3. `tflint --recursive --config .tflint.hcl` -> exit 0 (confirms no unused-var after removing root vars)
4. `trivy config . --severity HIGH,CRITICAL --exit-code 1` -> **passes** (no AWS-0132)
5. `terraform test -filter=tests/validations.tftest.hcl -filter=tests/plan.tftest.hcl` -> creds-free
   (mock_provider); confirms module + those tests still green
6. Apply tests (`deploy`, `parallel`) need `AWS_PROFILE=sandbox` (eu-west-1). Not run in CI. Offer to
   run the full suite against the sandbox as a final confidence check.

## Land it
- Stage the changed files + pending working-tree edits.
- **Amend** the single PR commit `b384bf0` (feature PR = one clean commit). Body stays accurate
  ("conditional SSE-KMS" still true) - no body change. Lines already <=100.
- `git push --force-with-lease`; poll `gh pr checks` until `terraform-module-testing` is green.

## Out of scope (surfaced, not done)
- Root `providers.tf` still uses `>=` for aws/core; per the same best practice a root should use `~>`.
  Left untouched (demoed centerpiece, outside "fix what you added"); flag as a follow-up.

input.plan:
# Plan - Fix CI on PR #33 - always-encrypt + documented trivy exception

## Context

PR #33 (`bgauduch/terraform-examples`, branch `feat/terraform-module-testing`) adds the live's
`terraform test` example. Two of three red checks already fixed + pushed (commit `b384bf0`):
commitlint (body reflow), fmt, deferred-actions/02 (transient 503), and the example's tflint
findings (`tests/setup` version constraints -> root-style `~>`; `enable_encryption` now used).

**Remaining red check:** `validate + lint + scan (terraform-module-testing)` fails at **trivy**:
single finding **AWS-0132 (HIGH)** - the example bucket isn't encrypted with a customer-managed key
(the module makes SSE-KMS conditional; with default vars there's no encryption at all).

## Empirical validation (trivy 0.71.2, probed locally)
- A managed key does NOT satisfy AWS-0132: `AES256` (SSE-S3) FAILS, `aws:kms` with no key id
  (aws/s3 managed key) FAILS; only `aws:kms` + a real CMK ARN PASSES.
- An inline `#trivy:ignore:AWS-0132` on the `aws_s3_bucket_server_side_encryption_configuration`
  resource **survives the CI 2-step** (`trivy config -o json` then `trivy convert --exit-code 1`):
  A/B proven - without it gate exit=1, with it the finding is dropped from the JSON and gate exit=0.

## Decision (per user)
Pragmatic for the example - don't over-engineer:
1. **Always encrypt the bucket.** With a CMK (when `enable_encryption` + `kms_key_arn`) -> SSE-KMS
   with that key; otherwise fall back to the **AWS-managed key for S3** (`kms_master_key_id = null`).
   This is strictly better than today (today: no encryption when no key).
2. **Add a documented trivy exception** (`#trivy:ignore:AWS-0132`) - the fallback path uses a
   managed key, not a CMK, which AWS-0132 flags.
3. **Leave a `TODO(security)` comment** to harden later (require/provision a CMK, e.g. the
   "root provisions a CMK" pattern - kept as a future improvement, not done now).
4. **Break nothing else** - verify all examples + the demo are unaffected.

## Changes (minimal)

### `examples/terraform-module-testing/modules/s3-bucket/main.tf`
Make the SSE config always-on + add the exception. `local.kms_enabled` is kept (drives the CMK
choice + the `encryption_enabled` output); `enable_encryption`, its cross-variable validation, and
all other resources stay as-is.
```hcl
# The bucket is always encrypted. With a CMK (enable_encryption + kms_key_arn) it uses
# SSE-KMS with that key; otherwise it falls back to the AWS-managed key for S3
# (kms_master_key_id = null). trivy AWS-0132 wants a customer-managed key, which the
# fallback path doesn't use, so it is ignored here.
# TODO(security): require a CMK to satisfy AWS-0132 for real - e.g. provision one in the
# root and inject it (like the deferred-actions example). Deferred hardening.
#trivy:ignore:AWS-0132
resource "aws_s3_bucket_server_side_encryption_configuration" "this" {
  bucket = aws_s3_bucket.this.id
  rule {
    apply_server_side_encryption_by_default {
      sse_algorithm     = "aws:kms"
      kms_master_key_id = local.kms_enabled ? var.kms_key_arn : null
    }
    bucket_key_enabled = true
  }
}
```
(was `count = local.kms_enabled ? 1 : 0` + `kms_master_key_id = var.kms_key_arn`)

### `examples/terraform-module-testing/tests/plan.tftest.hcl`
The SSE resource is no longer count-indexed, so the two encryption runs change from
presence/absence to key-id value (exact `rule` indexing verified by running the test):
- `no_encryption_block_without_key` -> `falls_back_to_managed_key_without_cmk`
  (`enable_encryption=false`): assert
  `one(aws_s3_bucket_server_side_encryption_configuration.this.rule).apply_server_side_encryption_by_default[0].kms_master_key_id == null`.
- `encryption_block_present_with_key` -> `uses_provided_cmk` (`enable_encryption=true`,
  `kms_key_arn="arn:...demo-key"`): assert the same path `== "arn:...demo-key"` (drop the
  `length(...) == 1` assertion).

### `examples/terraform-module-testing/tests/deploy.tftest.hcl`
`output.encryption_enabled == false` still holds (no CMK in use by default). Only update the
`error_message` wording to "CMK encryption must be off (managed-key fallback) ..." for accuracy.

### `examples/terraform-module-testing/modules/s3-bucket/outputs.tf`
Clarify the `encryption_enabled` description ("true when a customer-managed key is in use").

### Untouched
Module `variables.tf` (incl. the `enable_encryption` cross-var validation),
`tests/validations.tftest.hcl`, `tests/parallel.tftest.hcl`, root `main.tf`/`variables.tf`/
`outputs.tf`, `sweep.sh` (no real CMK is created anywhere -> no KMS leak -> sweeper stays S3-only).
User's pending working-tree edits (`~>` constraints, EN comments) kept.

## Verification (local)
1. `terraform init`; `terraform fmt -recursive -check` -> clean.
2. `tflint --recursive --config .tflint.hcl` -> exit 0.
3. CI 2-step: `trivy config . -f json -o trivy.json --exit-code 0` then
   `trivy convert --severity HIGH,CRITICAL --exit-code 1 trivy.json` -> **exit 0** (AWS-0132 ignored,
   no other HIGH).
4. `terraform test -filter=tests/validations.tftest.hcl -filter=tests/plan.tftest.hcl` -> creds-free
   (mock_provider); confirms the rewritten assertions hold and validations still pass.
5. Apply tests (`deploy`, `parallel`) need `AWS_PROFILE=sandbox` (eu-west-1); not in CI. Offer to run
   the full suite against the sandbox as a final check.

## Land it
- Stage changes + pending working-tree edits; **amend** the single PR commit `b384bf0` (body still
  accurate, lines <=100); `git push --force-with-lease`; poll `gh pr checks` until green.

## Out of scope (surfaced)
- Root `providers.tf` still `>=` for aws/core (best practice: `~>` for a root) - follow-up.
- The "root provisions a CMK" hardening (A1) - the proper AWS-0132 fix - deferred per the TODO.

```hcl
# fallback path doesn't use, so it is ignored here.
# TODO(security): require a CMK to satisfy AWS-0132 for real - e.g. provision one in the
# root and inject it (like the deferred-actions example). Deferred hardening.
#trivy:ignore:AWS-0132
resource "aws_s3_bucket_server_side_encryption_configuration" "this" {
  bucket = aws_s3_bucket.this.id
  rule {
    apply_server_side_encryption_by_default {
      sse_algorithm     = "aws:kms"
      kms_master_key_id = local.kms_enabled ? var.kms_key_arn : null
    }
    bucket_key_enabled = true
  }
}
```
(was `count = local.kms_enabled ? 1 : 0` + `kms_master_key_id = var.kms_key_arn`)

### `examples/terraform-module-testing/tests/plan.tftest.hcl`
The SSE resource is no longer count-indexed, so the two encryption runs change from
presence/absence to key-id value (exact `rule` indexing verified by running the test):
- `no_encryption_block_without_key` -> `falls_back_to_managed_key_without_cmk`
  (`enable_encryption=false`): assert
  `one(aws_s3_bucket_server_side_encryption_configuration.this.rule).apply_server_side_encryption_by_default[0].kms_master_key_id == null`.
- `encryption_block_present_with_key` -> `uses_provided_cmk` (`enable_encryption=true`,
  `kms_key_arn="arn:...demo-key"`): assert the same path `== "arn:...demo-key"` (drop the
  `length(...) == 1` assertion).

### `examples/terraform-module-testing/tests/deploy.tftest.hcl`
`output.encryption_enabled == false` still holds (no CMK in use by default). Only update the
`error_message` wording to "CMK encryption must be off (managed-key fallback) ..." for accuracy.

### `examples/terraform-module-testing/modules/s3-bucket/outputs.tf`
Clarify the `encryption_enabled` description ("true when a customer-managed key is in use").

### Untouched
Module `variables.tf` (incl. the `enable_encryption` cross-var validation),
`tests/validations.tftest.hcl`, `tests/parallel.tftest.hcl`, root `main.tf`/`variables.tf`/
`outputs.tf`, `sweep.sh` (no real CMK is created anywhere -> no KMS leak -> sweeper stays S3-only).
User's pending working-tree edits (`~>` constraints, EN comments) kept.

## Verification (local)
1. `terraform init`; `terraform fmt -recursive -check` -> clean.
2. `tflint --recursive --config .tflint.hcl` -> exit 0.
3. CI 2-step: `trivy config . -f json -o trivy.json --exit-code 0` then
   `trivy convert --severity HIGH,CRITICAL --exit-code 1 trivy.json` -> **exit 0** (AWS-0132 ignored,
   no other HIGH).
4. `terraform test -filter=tests/validations.tftest.hcl -filter=tests/plan.tftest.hcl` -> creds-free
   (mock_provider); confirms the rewritten assertions hold and validations still pass.
5. Apply tests (`deploy`, `parallel`) need `AWS_PROFILE=sandbox` (eu-west-1); not in CI. Offer to run
   the full suite against the sandbox as a final check.

## Land it
- Stage changes + pending working-tree edits; **amend** the single PR commit `b384bf0` (body still
  accurate, lines <=100); `git push --force-with-lease`; poll `gh pr checks` until green.

## Out of scope (surfaced)
- Root `providers.tf` still `>=` for aws/core (best practice: `~>` for a root) - follow-up.
- The "root provisions a CMK" hardening (A1) - the proper AWS-0132 fix - deferred per the TODO.

content.text: Gate exit 0. Le WARN "No enabled scanners" sur `convert` est un quirk connu - je vérifie que le scan a bien tourné (findings présentes, AWS-0132 = 0) et que ce n'est pas un scan vide.

input.command:
trivy config . --format json --output /tmp/tv.json --exit-code 0 --misconfig-scanners terraform 2>/dev/null && python3 -c '
import json
d=json.load(open("/tmp/tv.json"))
total=0; high=0; ids={}
a0132=0
for r in d.get("Results",[]):
    for m in r.get("Misconfigurations",[]):
        total+=1
        sev=m.get("Severity")
        if sev in ("HIGH","CRITICAL"): high+=1
        if m.get("ID")=="AWS-0132": a0132+=1
        ids[m.get("ID")]=ids.get(m.get("ID"),0)+1
print("total misconfigs found:", total, "(scan non-empty =", total>0, ")")
print("HIGH/CRITICAL count:", high)
print("AWS-0132 occurrences:", a0132, "(must be 0 = ignored)")
print("by id:", dict(sorted(ids.items())))
'; rm -f /tmp/tv.json

fix(terraform-module-testing): always-encrypt bucket + documented trivy AWS-0132 exception

The S3 module now always configures SSE-KMS: a customer-managed key when provided,
else the AWS-managed key for S3 (kms_master_key_id = null). AWS-0132 wants a CMK,
which the fallback path lacks, so it is ignored inline with a TODO to require a CMK
later (provision one in the root, like the deferred-actions example). Plan tests now
assert the encryption rule's key id instead of resource presence/absence.

content.text:
Fix committée (`66a25ec`, mes 4 fichiers uniquement; ton WIP Makefile/README intact). Les pre-commit gates sont passés au commit.

**Vérifications locales (toutes vertes):**
- `terraform fmt` ✓ · `tflint` exit 0 ✓
- **trivy 2-step CI** ✓ - AWS-0132 supprimée (0 occurrence dans le JSON), 0 HIGH/CRITICAL, scan non-vide (trouve AWS-0089 logging LOW non-gated)
- `terraform test` creds-free: **9/9** (5 validations dont `encryption_requires_kms_key`, 4 plan)

**Ce que fait la fix** (`modules/s3-bucket/main.tf`): SSE-KMS toujours actif - CMK si fournie, sinon `kms_master_key_id = null` → clé AWS-managée S3. `#trivy:ignore:AWS-0132` + commentaire `TODO(security)` pour durcir plus tard (CMK au root, pattern deferred-actions gardé pour plus tard). Tests plan ré-assertent la règle de chiffrement (key-id) au lieu de présence/absence. Aucune CMK créée → `sweep.sh` reste S3-only, rien cassé.

**Tests apply (`deploy`/`parallel`)**: non lancés (creds sandbox requis, hors CI). Analyse: défaut root `enable_encryption=false` → `encryption_enabled=false` (assertion inchangée OK), buckets chiffrés clé managée, aucune KMS créée → devraient passer. Je peux les lancer contre le sandbox si tu veux la confiance end-to-end.

**Décision push:** la branche est en avance de **5 commits** sur origin (tes 4 non-pushés + ma fix), fast-forward. Pousser déclenche la CI (devrait passer au vert). Ton WIP Makefile/README reste local (non poussé).

Je push maintenant, ou tu préfères finir ton Makefile/README d'abord et pousser toi-même ? (Hors-scope noté: `providers.tf` root encore en `>=`, à passer en `~>` plus tard.)

- [CRM - refus d'opportunité = règle de PROPOSITION](feedback_crm_refus_oppo_phrasing.md) — refus assumé Baptiste : proposer `feeling: positif` + `lost-reason` verbatim (no-go perso → `scope-changed`, jamais forcer esn-margin/tech-fit), MAIS surfacer/valider, jamais auto-appliquer en silence
- [Scope-discipline - exécuter seulement la liste validée](feedback_scope_discipline_endorsed_actions.md) — n'exécuter que les actions demandées ; mémoire rappelée = contexte pas mandat ; surfacer toute inférence avant write ; préserver la trace par défaut
- [CRM review - lentille analytics-readiness funnel](feedback_crm_review_analytics_lens.md) — toute review du modèle CRM doit enrichir les 3 personas avec une lentille analytics (requêtes funnel/leak/cohort calculables ?)
- [Obsidian - ne jamais utiliser <word> non-quoté](feedback_obsidian_html_tags.md) — `<vide>`, `<slug>`, etc. stripés en vue lecture (HTML), wrap dans backticks ou utiliser `(vide)`
- [CRM - backup prospect-type peu fiable](feedback_crm_backup_prospect_type_unreliable.md) — le champ `prospect-type` du Lead backup pre-split est parfois faux (cas Agicap "Client Final" → ESN), demander à l'utilisateur en cas de doute
- [Migration data - 3 couches garantie obligatoires](feedback_data_migration_guarantee_layers.md) — toute migration/refacto modèle data : énumérer Structurel / Schéma sémantique / Métier séparément, jamais les fusionner
- [Tracking files - MAJ proactive à chaque arbitrage/milestone](feedback_tracking_files_always_uptodate.md) — proposer activement la MAJ des plans/status/cold-start après toute décision tranchée ou changement de scope, sans attendre que l'utilisateur le demande
- [YAML frontmatter - jamais de ` : ` non-quoté dans texte libre](feedback_yaml_colon_in_text.md) — casse parse en mapping, validator skip, FK orphelines cascade. Préférer ` - ` ou block scalar `>-`. Run validate-crm.py avant tout commit massif.
- [Validator hardening - "0 erreur" ≠ couverture complète](feedback_validator_hardening_audit_periphery.md) — auditer le périmètre du validator avant de s'y fier (bypass silencieux : FK nested, enum drift, required absent, types incorrects)
- [DataviewJS render - settings OFF par défaut + reload Obsidian](feedback_dataviewjs_render_settings.md) — bloc dataviewjs en code raw = enableJavaScriptQueries OFF (défaut sécurité). Reload requis après activation. Patterns API (clés kebab→snake, Luxon, Link object)
- [ADRs MADR-lite vault-wide](feedback_adrs_madr_lite_vault.md) — décisions structurantes tracées en ADRs MADR-lite dans <aire>/ADRs/. Établi CRM + Éditorial. Numéros immutables, critères structurant/surprenant/coûteux. Proposer le pattern aux aires sans ADRs/
- [PARA archivage - extraire forward-looking AVANT git mv](feedback_para_archivage_extract_forward_looking.md) — backlog actif vers nouveau projet, doc vivante vers Area, décisions vers ADRs, historique reste archive. Propager wikilinks orphelins
- [git mv + Edit séquencement - re-git-add après Edits](feedback_git_mv_then_edit_restage.md) — git mv stage le rename, Edit modifie working tree post-staging. Re-faire git add new-path pour capturer le delta avant commit
- [Vault commit - inspecter l'index avant git commit](feedback_vault_commit_check_staged.md) — l'auto-backup stage tout en fond ; git status avant commit, sinon le commit embarque renames/sessions parallèles. Mieux : commit ciblé par pathspec `git commit -- <paths>` (ne committe QUE ces chemins quels que soient les fichiers stagés ; vérifier via `git show --stat`). Récup : reset --soft + restore --staged + re-add ciblé

- [Obsidian - wikilink note→note vs backtick fichier non-note](feedback_obsidian_reference_links.md) — ne pas convertir aveuglément les `[[X]]` en chemins ; wikilink si la cible est une vraie note, backtick si fichier non-note (schema.yml/.py) ou nom ambigu (CLAUDE.md)

content.subject: Gouvernance identité de série live tech_ (marque + titre, Option 3)
content.description: Passe dédiée post-live. Gouvernance de l'identité de série (visuelle ET textuelle), Option 3 décidée 2026-06-24. (A) MARQUE visuelle ❯ live tech_ : canoniser en §5.5 (markup HTML+CSS unique, vocab .series-mark/.sm-prompt/.sm-hl/.sm-cursor), élément FIXE dans event-banner/carrousel/stream/slides (unifier classes divergentes carrousel .series/.stk/.cur vs stream .label/.hl/.cursor ; ajouter event-banner + auditer slides), skills slots-only. (B) CONVENTION DE TITRE des lives : titre = `❯ live tech_ · <sujet>`, documentée en §5.5 (sibling textuel de la marque), add-editorial-piece (format live) préfixe auto, note Éditorial/CLAUDE.md + commentaire template piece. (C) ADR commun (décision structurante) + check lint (squelettes portent le bloc canonique ; titres format:live conformes) + bump design-system version + journal README brand. Drift constaté 2026-06-24 : event-banner sans la marque, classes divergentes, titres ad-hoc.
